comments:
Resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any way in
the employment selection process
must be retained in accordance with
AALL205.

series_title:
Notary Public Application and
Appointment Files
series_description:
This records series documents the application
and appointment files of an employee per 1
TAC 87.44: Qualification by an Officer or State
Employee who does not Furnish a Notary
Public Bond.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
NOTE: The Notary Public holds the
original certificate and retains control
of the records of notarization. If records
of notarization are left in a department,
they must be retained as state records
for three years after the last
notarization record and must be
produced for open records requests.
Records of notarization are not the
university’s records and will not require
authorization for disposition from the
RMO.

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in
the computation of taxes.

Documentation used to start, modify, or stop
all voluntary or required deductions from
payroll, including garnishment or other court-
ordered attachments. Includes savings bonds.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes Wage and Tax Statement Form (W-2),
1098, 1099, Federal Insurance Contributions
Act (FICA), and others.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew, overtime, or case
schedules, rosters, or assignments.

Requests and authorizations for work-related
activities for which the expenses of the
employee are defrayed or reimbursed. Records
in this series may include: requests for travel
authorizations (RTA) and requests for travel
reimbursements (RTR); requests for
participation in educational programs,
workshops, college classes, or other activities;
and related documentation and
correspondence.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Departments must retain
any original documentation that is
required but not submitted to central
imaging. CAUTION: Records for
sponsored project grant records may
require additional retention, per
agreement with sponsoring
organization. Refer to research-related
series.

series_description:
This series documents internally distributed
manuals, guidelines, or similar records that
define university policies and procedures
concerning university personnel. Records in
this series include development files and
related correspondence.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
Refer to AALL058 for non-personnel
university rules, policies, and
procedures, and to AALL587 for general
office procedures and guides.

series_description:
Aptitude or skills tests required of job
applicants or of current personnel to qualify for
promotion or transfer.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each different
test (different in terms of either
questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for the
period indicated. An applicant's test
results must be retained as part of
AALL205 employment selection

series_title:
Training Materials and
Training Administration
Records
series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
associated with training university members
and affiliates on policies, procedures, and rules
that govern the institution's programs,
services, or projects. Includes records
documenting the planning, development,
implementation, scheduling, and evaluation of
in-house training programs including but not
limited to scheduling plans and training
matrices; presentations, user guides, training
manuals, syllabi, course outlines, and similar
training materials; and course registration,
class rosters, instructors, and sign-in sheets.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Training materials that are
not created in or added to UTLearn
must be retained by the department.
Refer to AALL232 for individual staff
training records, AALL077 for public-
facing guides, AALL587 for office
procedures, and AALL683 for
hazardous material training records.

series_description:
Time and attendance records documenting
individual employee’s hours worked, including
work schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal hours
(i.e., overtime or less-than-full-time worked)
for those employees working on fixed
schedules.
comments:
Timesheets created prior to or external
to Workday or UT Direct Time Reports
must be retained and disposed by the
department. Refer to AALL308 Leave
Management Records for time-off
requests or correspondence (e.g.,
emails, texts, chats, etc.) requesting,
documenting, or authorizing time off.

series_title:
Time Cards and Timesheets –
Work-Study Students
series_description:
This series documents hours worked by work-
study student employees. The series is used for
payroll purposes and to meet federal
requirements for documenting time worked by
work-study students. This series may include
but is not limited to: Work-Study Time
Certificates and referrals, time cards, and
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Refer to AALL306.1 for all other time
cards and timesheets, including those
of non-work-study student employees.

series_description:
This series documents Requests for Outside
Employment forms created outside the UT
System Outside Activity Portal, and conflict of
interest, conflict of commitment, and outside
activities documentation held by university
departments.
comments:
For conflicts of interest related to
research, refer to AALL327.
legal_citation:
Regents' Rules and
Regulations, Section
30104; UT Austin HOP 2-
2220 and 5-2011

series_description:
Employee requests and supervisor
authorizations or denials for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical Leave
Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and other types of
authorized leave. Includes supporting
departmental documentation, related reports,
and correspondence.
comments:
Emails, texts, or chats requesting and
authorizing time off are considered
transitory once the timesheet is signed.
Denied requests should be kept for the
duration of the retention period. Use
AALL306.1 or AALL306.2 for
timesheets. CAUTION: You must be
able to produce non-medical employee
records separately from employee
medical records or information. All
employee records must be secured to
protect privacy of the employee.
legal_citation:
UT Austin HOP Leave
Policies; TGC 661; 29
CFR 825.500(b)

series_title:
Flexible Work Arrangements
and Telecommuting
Agreements
series_description:
The records in this series include flexible work
arrangement (FWA) and telecommuting forms,
requests, and agreements submitted by the
employee and either approved or denied by
the department.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Documents created prior to or external
to Workday must be retained by the
department or uploaded to Workday
for retention. These records may be
retained as part of departmental
employee folder, AALL207.

series_title:
Program Application
Materials for Applicants Who
Do Not Enter
series_description:
This series documents the application process
for individuals seeking admission to graduate
or undergraduate programs with admissions
requirements separate and beyond those of
the institution.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Use AALL358 Departmental
Student Records for the admissions
records for students who matriculate.
NOTE: For use by departments other
than the Office of Admissions.

series_description:
This series is used to provide a record of the
administration of student internship,
practicum, capstone, and cooperative
education programs. Programs may be within
the university or off campus and for class credit
and/or pay. Records may include but are not
limited to: applications for internships inside
and external to the institution, agreements
with departments and sponsors,
postings/notices, student resumes and
transcripts, notes, and related documentation
and correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Records of a student's participation in
an internship program must be
retained as part of AALL358.

series_description:
Applications for internship, practicum, and
cooperative education programs for which the
student did not enroll.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Refer to AALL313 for applications for
students who enroll.

series_description:
This series documents grievances brought
forward by students against the institution that
do not result in litigation. Grievances may
pertain to academic issues, housing,
affirmative action and equal opportunity,
student conduct, and other issues. Records
may include but are not limited to: notices of
grievance; informal discussion notes; grievance
responses; formal hearing notes, including
audio tapes; final summary statements;
settlement agreements; appeals
documentation; and related documents and
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
If subject to litigation, refer to AALL079;
if mediated informally, refer to
OMB001. Portions of these records
may be exempt from public disclosure.
NOTE: These records may be retained
as part of AALL358 departmental
student records.
legal_citation:
University General
Information Catalog:
Student Grievance
Procedures

series_title:
Transportation of Hazardous
Materials (other than
Hazardous Waste)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Shipping papers for
hazardous waste must be retained for 3
years after acceptance by carrier; refer
to AALL323.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Records required for Department of Defense
program to facilitate secure communications
and identity/encryption authentications to DoD
systems
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years and 6 months

series_description:
Documentation maintained by the Principal
Investigator (PI). Includes: proposal, award and
agreement records, research documentation,
research data, financial records, and other
documentation created through grant
administration and reporting.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Refer also to OSP552 Sponsored
Research Contracts/Grants Files and
AALL334 for research protocols and
approvals. CAUTION: Contract terms
may specify additional retention
requirements for research data.

series_title:
Research Grant Records –
Final Research Report
series_description:
These records relate to final research reports
given to a granting agency or sponsor for grant-
funded or sponsored research projects
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Final research report for research
involving human subjects must be
retained for at least 3 years and in
accordance with related IRB records;
refer to OSP552

series_description:
Research-related conflict of interest disclosure
forms.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Limited Submissions –
Department, College, School,
or Unit Documentation
series_description:
Documentation retained by the nominating
department for foundation/limited award
research grant opportunities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Institutionally Funded
Research – Project Review
Records
series_description:
Documentation of the administrative activities
of the institutional councils and boards, which
review proposals and make recommendations
for awards to faculty for institutionally funded
research. This series may include but is not
limited to: applicant case files; agendas; notes;
working papers; award letters; and applications
for research support.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Institutionally Funded
Research – Minutes and Final
Research Reports
series_description:
Documentation of the decisions and final
actions of the institutional councils and boards,
which review proposals and make
recommendations for awards to faculty for
institutionally funded research. This series
includes meeting minutes and final research
reports.
retention_statement: Permanent
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: All records and reports shall
be maintained for at least three years
and in accordance with related IACUC
records. NOTE: Whenever the
Administrator notifies a research
facility in writing that specified records
shall be retained pending completion of
an investigation or proceeding under
the Act, the research facility shall hold
those records until their disposition is
authorized in writing by the
Administrator
legal_citation:
9 CFR 2.35(f); Public
Health Services Policy

Use of Laboratory
Animals (D.L. 99-158)

series_title:
Animal Health Records for
Species Not Covered by the
Animal Welfare Act and
Rodents
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
legal_citation:
9 CFR 2.35(f); Public
Health Services Policy
on Humane Care and
Use of Laboratory
Animals (D.L. 99-158)

series_title:
Animal Health Records for
Non-Rodent Species Covered
by the Animal Welfare Act
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
legal_citation:
9 CFR 2.35(f); Public
Health Services Policy
on Humane Care and

Animals (D.L. 99-158)

series_title:
Protocols for Research
Projects Involving Human and
Animal Subjects
series_description:
Human Subject: Protocol applications, research
protocols, consent documents, recruitment
materials, investigator brochures, and all other
documents submitted for review of proposed
human subject research; Scientific evaluations,
when provided by an entity other than the IRB;
IRB meeting minutes; Progress reports
submitted by researchers; Reports of injuries to
participants; Data and safety monitoring
reports, if any; Modifications to previously
approved research; Records of continuing
review activities; Unanticipated problems
involving risks to subjects or others;
Documentation of non-compliance; Significant
new findings; correspondence between the IRB
and researchers Animal Subjects: Protocol
applications and all supporting documentation
reviewed by the IACUC; IACUC meeting
minutes; adverse event reports;
correspondence between the IACUC and
researchers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Records for research involving human
and animal subjects must be retained
for at least 3 years and in accordance
with related IRB records; refer to
AALL325 Research Grant
Administration Records and OSP552
Sponsored Research Contracts/Grants
Files.

information. Refer to DSO379 or
DSO381.

series_title:
UT Testing Center – Test
Administration Records
series_description:
This series provides a record of the services
rendered to clients for national, state, and local
tests. Tests administered may include the
Graduate Record Examination (GRE), Praxis I,
Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL),
Course Credit, Credit by Exam (CBE), State of
Texas licensing and credentialing exams, and
other exams for partner organizations. Records
may include but are not limited to: applications
to take tests, testing rules and regulations,
inventory logs, rosters of test takers, seating
charts, supervisors' reports, and vouchers for
payment of testing. This series does not include
test scores, answer sheets, or results
documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Testing centers must retain
records of testing according to the
procedures and requirements
established by the contracting
organization. Testing materials, test
scores, and results documentation are
administered by the organization that
has oversight for each test. Refer to
TES102 for course credit and CBE
testing materials and rosters and to
TES103 for CBE answer sheets.

series_title:
Course Credit and Credit by
Exam (CBE) – Testing
Materials and Rosters
series_description:
This series provides a record of test materials
and rosters of students for course credit and
credit by exam tests administered by the
testing center. Records include tests, test
response sheets, test keys, inventory logs or
similar records of test booklets or other testing
instruments over which control is necessary to
protect the security and integrity of the test,
and rosters of test takers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 15 years

series_title:
Course Credit and Credit by
